title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
The continuous Fourier transform of a function \(f(t)\) is:
\[
\hat{f}(\xi) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t)\, e^{-2\pi i \xi t}\, dt
\]
And the inverse:
\[
f(t) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \hat{f}(\xi)\, e^{2\pi i \xi t}\, d\xi
\]

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
The continuous Fourier transform of a function \(f(t)\) is:
\[
\hat{f}(\xi) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t)\, e^{-2\pi i \xi t}\, dt
\]
And the inverse:
\[
f(t) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \hat{f}(\xi)\, e^{2\pi i \xi t}\, d\xi
\]

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
Key properties



Property
Time domain
Frequency domain




Linearity
\(\alpha f + \beta g\)
\(\alpha \hat{f} + \beta \hat{g}\)


Time shift
\(f(t - t_0)\)
\(e^{-2\pi i \xi t_0} \hat{f}(\xi)\)


Convolution
\((f * g)(t)\)
\(\hat{f}(\xi) \cdot \hat{g}(\xi)\)


Parseval
\(\int |f|^2\, dt\)
\(\int |\hat{f}|^2\, d\xi\)

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
Visualisation
The animation below (rendered in Manim) shows how a square wave decomposes into a sum of sinusoids as more Fourier terms are added:



Fourier series decomposition








Note



Replace images/fourier.gif with your own Manim output. Any .gif or .mp4 in the images/ folder works — no extra config needed.

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
Worked example — Gaussian
For \(f(t) = e^{-\pi t^2}\), the Fourier transform is its own Gaussian:
\[
\hat{f}(\xi) = e^{-\pi \xi^2}
\]
This is the only function (up to scaling) that is an eigenfunction of the Fourier transform with eigenvalue 1.
If we want it to have normalization one, we then need to define: \[
\hat{f}(\xi) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2 \pi \sigma^2}} e^{-\pi \xi^2}
\] —

Enter a parametric curve \(\vec{r}(t) = (x(t),\, y(t),\, z(t))\) as three comma-separated expressions in \(t\), then click Plot. Use the slider to move the TNB frame along the curve.

▶ Play  0.00

title: Notes — Fourier Transform
text:
Code block (optional)
You can also include executable Python code cells if you have Jupyter installed:

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

t = np.linspace(-3, 3, 1000)
f = np.exp(-np.pi * t**2)
xi = np.fft.fftfreq(len(t), d=t[1]-t[0])
fhat = np.fft.fft(f) * (t[1]-t[0])

plt.plot(np.fft.fftshift(xi), np.abs(np.fft.fftshift(fhat)))
plt.xlabel(r"$\xi$")
plt.title("Magnitude spectrum")
plt.show()

title: Manim External
text:
This launches a full Jupyter environment in the cloud via Binder, where you can run the Manim TNB frame animation and edit the curve \(\vec{r}(t)\) directly in a notebook cell.






Note



First launch takes 1–2 minutes while Binder builds the environment. Subsequent launches are faster.





The notebook opens automatically
Edit r_sym and the t_start / t_end values in Cell 2
Click Run All (or press Shift+Enter through each cell)
The animation renders and plays inline



 🚀 Launch Manim Notebook 















Curve
r_sym




Default (Lissajous)
s.Matrix([2*cos(3*t), 2*sin(t), 2*cos(2*t)])


Helix
s.Matrix([cos(t), sin(t), 0.3*t])


Trefoil knot
s.Matrix([sin(t)+2*sin(2*t), cos(t)-2*cos(2*t), -sin(3*t)])


Torus knot
s.Matrix([(2+cos(3*t))*cos(2*t), (2+cos(3*t))*sin(2*t), sin(3*t)])

title: About
text:
This site is built with Quarto and hosted on GitHub Pages.
It’s a place for math notes, Manim animations, and anything else worth writing down.

title: Home
text: Welcome to my site. Here you’ll find math notes, animations, and write-ups.

title: Home
text:
Quick example — inline LaTeX
The quadratic formula is \(x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}\).
Euler’s identity: \(e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0\)

title: Home
text:
Display math
Maxwell’s equations in differential form:
\[
\nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} = \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0}
\]
\[
\nabla \times \mathbf{B} = \mu_0 \mathbf{J} + \mu_0 \varepsilon_0 \frac{\partial \mathbf{E}}{\partial t}
\]
\[
\nabla \cdot \mathbf{B}=0
\] \[
\nabla \times \mathbf{E}=-\frac{\partial \mathbf{B}}{dt}
\]

title: Home
text:
Embedding a GIF
Drop your Manim-rendered GIF into an images/ folder next to this file, then embed it like this:
![Manim animation description](images/your-animation.gif)
With a caption and sizing:
![Double pendulum simulation](images/pendulum.gif){fig-align="center" width="70%"}
For .mp4 videos use the Quarto video shortcode:

title: TNB Frame Animation
text:
Edit r_sym in the cell below to define your parametric curve, then run all cells.
The animation will render and play inline.

from manim import *
import sympy as s
import numpy as np
from sympy import symbols, sqrt, lambdify, sin, cos, tan, exp, log, pi

t = symbols('t')


# ── Edit your curve here ──────────────────────────────────────────
r_sym = s.Matrix([2*cos(3*t), 2*sin(t), 2*cos(2*t)])

# t range for the animation
t_start = 0
t_end   = 2 * float(pi)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


%%manim -qm TNBFrameclass TNBFrame(ThreeDScene):    def construct(self):        def TNB_get(func):            v_sym = func.diff(t)            speed_sym = sqrt(v_sym.dot(v_sym))            T_sym = v_sym / speed_sym            N_sym = T_sym.diff(t) / sqrt(T_sym.diff(t).dot(T_sym.diff(t)))            B_sym = T_sym.cross(N_sym)            T = lambdify(t, T_sym, 'numpy')            N = lambdify(t, N_sym, 'numpy')            B = lambdify(t, B_sym, 'numpy')            return T, N, B        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_length=12, y_length=10, z_length=8)        self.move_camera(phi=PI/3, theta=PI/4)        self.add(axes)        self.wait()        r_temp = lambdify(t, r_sym, 'numpy')        r_plot = lambda t: np.squeeze(r_temp(t))        C  = ParametricFunction(r_plot, t_range=[t_start, t_end], color=RED)        t0 = MathTex(r'\vec{r}(t)=', color=RED)        T_l = MathTex(r'\vec{T}', color=GREEN)        N_l = MathTex(r'\vec{N}', color=BLUE)        B_l = MathTex(r'\vec{B}', color=PURPLE)        TNB_l = VGroup(T_l, N_l, B_l)        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(t0.shift(3*UP + 3*RIGHT).scale(0.75))        self.play(Create(C), run_time=5)        self.wait()        tracker = ValueTracker(t_start)        T_f, N_f, B_f = TNB_get(r_sym)        T = lambda t: np.squeeze(T_f(t))        N = lambda t: np.squeeze(N_f(t))        B = lambda t: np.squeeze(B_f(t))        T_p = Vector(T(t_start), color=GREEN).shift(r_plot(t_start))        T_p.add_updater(lambda m: m.become(            Vector(T(tracker.get_value()), color=GREEN).shift(r_plot(tracker.get_value()))))        N_p = Vector(N(t_start), color=BLUE).shift(r_plot(t_start))        N_p.add_updater(lambda m: m.become(            Vector(N(tracker.get_value()), color=BLUE).shift(r_plot(tracker.get_value()))))        B_p = Vector(B(t_start), color=PURPLE).shift(r_plot(t_start))        B_p.add_updater(lambda m: m.become(            Vector(B(tracker.get_value()), color=PURPLE).shift(r_plot(tracker.get_value()))))        self.add(VGroup(T_p, N_p, B_p))        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(TNB_l.arrange(DOWN).next_to(t0, 2*RIGHT))        self.wait()        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(t_end), run_time=10, rate_func=linear)        self.wait()
